learning_outcomes.description: Understand the role of the creative team in campaign development and how creative advertising concepts are developed in response to client and creative briefs.
Analyse and critique creative strategies to assess the effectiveness of advertising campaigns, through identifying strengths and weaknesses in message strategy and execution styles.
Understand how to access and use creative thinking and apply that to practical idea generation and other processes involved in contemporary creative production and development.
Develop skills in idea generation, conceptualisation, design and production to develop impactful creative advertisements and promotional content for a variety of media-matched delivery platforms.
Demonstrate an understanding of strategic implications in the creative development process, to achieve strategic creative message objectives.

assessments.task_description:
Creativity is a critical driver of advertising effectiveness. Effective advertising requires strategically grounded creative decisions that communicate a compelling message, resonate with the intended audience, and contribute to achieving defined communication objectives.

A creative strategy defines the key strategic choices required to develop and effectively deliver a targeted message to a desired consumer segment. It establishes what a brand wishes to communicate, how it seeks to influence audience perceptions, and the creative approach used to achieve those objectives. Developing an effective advertising campaign requires the integration of a strong message strategy, appropriate advertising appeals, impactful execution techniques, and consideration of the broader context in which advertising operates.

In this assessment, you are required to select an existing advertising campaign* for a product or service and critically analyse its creative strategy, message strategy, and execution style. Drawing on relevant creative strategy theory and secondary research, you will evaluate the relationship between creativity, strategy, effectiveness, and the broader ethical, cultural, and societal implications of advertising practice.

*Note: Content in the advertisement must be in English and the product or brand selected must be familiar to the teaching staff. In addition, the campaign must have been active within the past two to three years.

Your analysis should be supported by credible secondary research and connections to creative strategy theory. You are required to evaluate the extent to which available evidence supports claims of campaign effectiveness, critically assess the strengths and weaknesses of the campaign's creative strategy and execution, and consider any relevant stakeholder, ethical, or societal implications. Based on your analysis, you will present recommendations for how the campaign could be improved to achieve greater effectiveness.

This is an individual assessment and students must not collaborate with other members of the cohort when preparing this written report. A detailed assessment guide outlining the report structure and requirements will be available on Blackboard. The analytical, evaluative, and strategic skills developed through this assessment will be further applied and extended in subsequent assessment items.

The Creative Strategy Analysis report word count is 1,500 words (±10%). The reference list and appendices are not included in the word count. All submissions must be written in English. Students are also required to submit an AI Disclosure Statement to support responsible AI use, transparency, and critical reflection. A detailed assessment guide and marking rubric are provided on Blackboard.
assessments.submission_guidelines: This assignment is to be submitted online through the submission link on Blackboard.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
In this assessment, you will build on the analytical skills developed in Assessment 1 to develop original and strategically informed advertising concepts and creative executions in response to a specified creative brief.

Beginning with an analysis of the creative brief, you will undertake research into the intended target audience to generate insights and identify opportunities for creative development. Drawing on creative strategy theory, audience understanding, and ideation techniques, you will develop a compelling Big Idea that informs the campaign's message strategy and creative execution.

You are required to respond to the creative brief by developing a Creative Strategy Portfolio that documents the strategic foundations of your proposed campaign, including audience insights, the Big Idea, message strategy, creative direction, and supporting concept development. The portfolio should demonstrate the integration of strategic thinking, creative problem-solving, and evidence-informed decision-making to develop creative solutions that effectively address campaign objectives and resonate with the intended audience.

Students will submit a Strategic Decision Log to explain, justify, and evaluate key strategic and creative decisions, including the evidence, insights, and reasoning underpinning the proposed campaign; and an AI Disclosure Statement to support responsible AI use, transparency, and critical reflection. The Strategic Decision Log will also include considerations for the broader stakeholder, ethical, cultural, and societal implications of their creative choices.

This is an individual assessment, and students must not collaborate with other members of the cohort when preparing this portfolio.

A step-by-step assessment brief outlining the portfolio requirements will be available in the assessment folder on Blackboard. Please refer to this guide for full details of the assessment.

The Creative Strategy Portfolio has a word-count equivalent of 2,500 words (±10%). The reference list and appendices are not included in the word count. All submissions must be written in English. A detailed assessment guide and marking rubric will be provided on Blackboard.
assessments.submission_guidelines: This assignment is to be submitted online through the submission link on Blackboard.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.assessment_title: Creative Strategy Campaign Development and Pitch
assessments.task_description:
In this final assessment, you will demonstrate your ability to integrate and apply the concepts, theories, and practices explored throughout the course to develop, refine, and present a strategically grounded and creatively compelling advertising campaign.

Building on the Creative Strategy Portfolio developed in Assessment 2, you will refine, execute, and present your integrated advertising campaign in response to the creative brief. Drawing on audience insights, creative strategy theory, and creative development processes, you will develop polished advertising executions across appropriate media platforms (e.g., print, multimedia, social media) that effectively communicate a persuasive message to the intended audience and support the achievement of campaign objectives. Students are expected to demonstrate how feedback, evaluation, and further development of their creative strategy informed the final campaign.

As part of this assessment, you will prepare and deliver a pre-recorded pitch presentation to persuasively sell-in your campaign concept. You will also develop a Creative Rationale that explains, justifies, and evaluates the strategic and creative decisions underpinning the campaign, including the evidence, insights, and reasoning used to support your recommendations. The rationale should demonstrate critical judgement regarding the relationship between creativity, effectiveness, and broader stakeholder, ethical, cultural, and societal considerations.

This is an individual assessment, and students must not collaborate with other members of the cohort when preparing this campaign.

A step-by-step assessment brief outlining the campaign requirements, pitch presentation expectations, and submission details will be available in the assessment folder on Blackboard. Please refer to this guide for full details of the assessment.

Students are also required to submit an AI Disclosure Statement to support responsible AI use, transparency, and critical reflection regarding the role of AI in contemporary creative practice.
assessments.submission_guidelines: This assignment is to be submitted online through the Blackboard Assessment link.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.
